prerequisites: A NetSuite account with administrator (or equivalent) access to enable features and create integration records and access tokens.
Enable the REST Web Services feature under Setup > Company > Enable Features > SuiteCloud (SuiteTalk Web Services section), accepting the SuiteCloud Terms of Service.
Enable the Token-Based Authentication (TBA) feature under Setup > Company > Enable Features > SuiteCloud (Manage Authentication section).
A role with the REST Web Services (Full) and Log in using Access Tokens (Full) permissions assigned to the user that will own the access token.
Your NetSuite Account ID, used as the realm config field, found at Setup > Company > Company Information.
A start_datetime value marking the earliest record modification date from which to begin syncing data.

authMethods.name: OAuth 1.0a Token-Based Authentication (TBA)
authMethods.summary: NetSuite REST Web Services authenticates with OAuth 1.0a token-based authentication. The connector requires five secret values: the integration record's consumer_key and consumer_secret, the access token's token_key and token_secret, and the realm (your NetSuite Account ID). Create the integration record under Setup > Integration > Manage Integrations to obtain the consumer key and secret, then create an access token under Setup > Users/Roles > Access Tokens to obtain the token key and secret.

setupSteps.title: Enable REST Web Services and Token-Based Authentication
setupSteps.body: In NetSuite go to Setup > Company > Enable Features. On the SuiteCloud subtab, enable REST Web Services (under the SuiteTalk Web Services section) and Token-Based Authentication (under the Manage Authentication section), accepting the SuiteCloud Terms of Service. Save.

setupSteps.title: Assign the required permissions to a role
setupSteps.body: Go to Setup > Users/Roles > Manage Roles and edit or customize a role. On the Permissions subtab under Setup, add REST Web Services with level Full and Log in using Access Tokens with level Full. Assign this role to the user that will own the access token. Avoid using the Administrator role for integrations.

setupSteps.title: Create an integration record (consumer key and secret)
setupSteps.body: Go to Setup > Integration > Manage Integrations > New. Enter a name, set State to Enabled, and check Token-Based Authentication on the Authentication tab. Save. NetSuite displays the Consumer Key and Consumer Secret only once. Copy them into the consumer_key and consumer_secret config fields.

setupSteps.title: Create an access token (token key and secret)
setupSteps.body: Go to Setup > Users/Roles > Access Tokens > New. Select the integration record (Application Name), the User, and the Role configured above. Save. NetSuite displays the Token ID and Token Secret only once. Copy them into the token_key and token_secret config fields.

setupSteps.title: Provide the Account ID and configure sync options
setupSteps.body: Find your Account ID at Setup > Company > Company Information and enter it in the realm config field. Set start_datetime to the earliest modification date to sync from. Optionally set object_types to limit which record types are extracted and window_in_days to control the size of each incremental sync window.
